description:
Batch 2/5 — Reactive Scheduling (2 of 2)

Extract all non-agent housekeeping from project_tick into a standalone housekeeping_tick() function:

Operations to extract:
- flush_git_integration_outbox
- bootstrap_from_vision_if_needed
- ensure_tasks_for_unplanned_requirements (ai_task_generation)
- resume_interrupted_workflows_for_project
- reconcile_stale_in_progress_tasks_for_project
- reconcile_dependency_gate_tasks_for_project
- reconcile_merge_gate_tasks_for_project
- retry_failed_task_workflows
- promote_backlog_tasks_to_ready
- refresh_runtime_binaries_if_main_advanced
- Collect summary metrics and emit daemon events

housekeeping_tick runs on --interval-secs timer (default 5s), independent of agent execution. After housekeeping discovers new ready tasks or resumed workflows, it signals the coordinator to check work_queue and backfill pool slots. The existing project_tick function can delegate to housekeeping_tick + pool operations, preserving backward compatibility for --once mode.
title: Separate housekeeping timer from agent execution in daemon run loop
